blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلصَّـٰٓفَّـٰتِ صَفًّا (۱)
By the [angels] ranged in ranks, (1)
فَٱلزَّٰجِرَٰتِ زَجْرًا (۲)
by the ones who drive [the clouds] vigorously, (2)
فَٱلتَّـٰلِيَـٰتِ ذِكْرًا (۳)
by the ones who recite the reminder: (3)
إِنَّ إِلَـٰهَكُمْ لَوَٰحِدٌ (٤)
indeed your God is certainly One, (4)
رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَرَبُّ ٱلْمَشَـٰرِقِ (٥)
the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and the Lord of the easts. (5)

Key Points

This Surah begins with three oaths sworn by Allah. Although Allah has no need for oaths, and the believers accept His word without them, an oath is a sign of the importance and greatness of the subject sworn upon, and serves to draw attention to it. Therefore, Allah has sworn by various matters. The intent of al-Saffat (those who line up), al-Zajirat (those who restrain), and al-Taliyat (those who recite) are the angels who execute Divine commands in organized ranks, restrain people from temptation, and are constantly mindful of Allah. However, a group has said that the intent refers to the warriors who defend the sanctity of truth in organized ranks and gain morale and strength through the recitation of Divine verses and the remembrance of Allah. The word al-Zajirat can be derived from zajr, meaning "to restrain," which refers to restraining from temptations and sins; and it can also be derived from zajrah, meaning "a shout" or "a cry," meaning an oath by the shouters (or heralds) of history.

Messages

1- In life, we must value purposeful order. (In the two Surahs, al-Saffat and al-Saff, order and discipline are discussed and sworn upon.) «و الصافّات صفّاً»

2- Order, unity, power, consensus, and cooperation, which are manifested in the rank (saff), are signs of the Divine agents. «و الصافّات صفّاً»

3- The existence of factors that restrain from sin and enjoin the forbidding of evil (nahy ‘anil-munkar) is necessary in society. «فالزاجرات زجرا»

4- Gaining energy from the remembrance of Allah through the recitation of the Quran «التالیات ذكرا», defending the sanctity of truth «الزاجرات», coupled with order and readiness, «و الصافّات صفّاً», is a necessity for a Divine system.

5- The execution of every task must be accompanied by high quality: the best order (saffan), the best power (zajran), and the best remembrance of Allah (dhikran).

6- We must proclaim the slogan of Monotheism (Tawhid) firmly, beautifully, and before anything else. (In these verses, the oath is first presented with a beautiful rhythm, and then:) «ان الهكم لواحد»

7- We should not swear oaths for everything. (Usually, the oaths in the Quran are for fundamental and important issues such as the Origin and the Return [Mabda’ and Ma’ad]).

In Surah al-Dhariyat, after several oaths, He says: «انّما توعدون لصادق»(2) (Surely, what you are promised is true). In Surah al-Mursalat, after several oaths, the discussion is about the Day of Judgment, where He says: «انّما توعدون لواقع»(3) (Surely, what you are promised will occur). And here, after several oaths, the discussion is about Monotheism, and He says: «انّ الهكم لواحد» (Yes, in the face of those who are strongly committed to polytheism [Shirk], successive oaths must be sworn).

8- The polytheists differentiated between the Divinity (Uluhiyyah) and the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah; therefore, this verse emphasizes the oneness of both. «الهكم لواحد ربّ السموات والارض»

9- The entire existence is under Divine nurturing and growth. «ربّ السموات والارض»

10- Do not imagine a separate Lord (Rabb) for everything, because the Lord of all existence is but one. «ربّ السموات والارض»

11- Among the signs and proofs of Monotheism is the harmony between the heavens, the earth, and the other creatures of the universe. «انّ الهكم لواحد ربّ السموات والارض...»

12- The Earth is spherical. (The word mashariq [plural of rising places] and the difference in sunrises and sunsets are signs of the Earth's sphericity).

13- The sun and its light play an effective role in human life. (Although the sun is part of the heavens and is implicitly included in «ربّ السموات», the specific mention of mashariq is a sign of the special importance of the sunrise). «و ربّ المشارق»

(2) Al-Dhariyat, 5. (3) Al-Mursalat, 7.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah al-Saffat

This Surah, consisting of 182 verses, was revealed in Mecca. The name of this Surah is derived from its first verse, which begins with an oath by al-Saffat (those who line up), referring to a group of angels who stand in ranks to execute the Divine command, as well as verse 165, where the word al-Saffun appears. Similar to this Surah in name is Surah al-Saff (The Rank), which refers to the firm ranks of warriors on the battlefields. This Surah is the first Surah in the Holy Quran that begins with an oath. Like other Meccan Surahs, most of the verses in this Surah concern the Origin and the Return (Mabda’ and Ma’ad) and are accompanied by admonition and warning. It also refers to the history of Prophets such as Noah, Abraham, Isaac, Moses, Aaron, Elias, Lot, and Jonah, with the life of Prophet Abraham being detailed more extensively among them. The false beliefs of the polytheists regarding Allah’s relationship with the jinn or the angels are strongly condemned, and the final victory of truth over disbelief (kufr), polytheism (shirk), and hypocrisy (nifaq) is discussed. Emphasis has been placed on reciting Surah al-Saffat on Fridays, and its recitation leads to an increase in sustenance (rizq) and the safeguarding of wealth and children from Satan. (1)

(1) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Ayahs

إِنَّا زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِزِينَةٍ ٱلْكَوَاكِبِ (٦)
Indeed We have adorned the lowest heaven with the finery of the stars, (6)
وَحِفْظًا مِّن كُلِّ شَيْطَـٰنٍ مَّارِدٍ (۷)
and to guard from any froward devil. (7)

Key Points

| "Mārid" (مارد) refers to something that has no goodness or blessing and is wicked (khabīth). For example, a tree without leaves is called shajar amrad.

Messages

1- The adornment of the heavens is a reflection of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّ السموات... انا زیّنا السماء»

2- The inclination towards adornment and beauty is among the innate human tendencies, and the Quran has affirmed it. «زیّنا السّماء الدنیا»

3- Adornment and beauty are fundamental principles. All of Allah's creations are beautiful. «زیّنا السّماء»

4- There are secrets in the higher realm that the devils (Shayāṭīn) attempt to steal, but they are unable to do so. «حفظاً من كل شیطان مارد»

5- The Jinn and the devilish Jinn (Shayāṭīn Jinnī) have the power to access the unseen (ghayb), but they are prevented from it. «حفظاً من كل شیطان مارد»

Ayahs

لَّا يَسَّمَّعُونَ إِلَى ٱلْمَلَإِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ وَيُقْذَفُونَ مِن كُلِّ جَانِبٍ (۸)
They do not eavesdrop on the Supernal Elite—they are shot at from every side, (8)
دُحُورًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ وَاصِبٌ (۹)
to drive them away, and there is a perpetual punishment for them— (9)
إِلَّا مَنْ خَطِفَ ٱلْخَطْفَةَ فَأَتْبَعَهُۥ شِهَابٌ ثَاقِبٌ (۱٠)
except any who snatches a snatch, whereat a piercing flame pursues him. (10)

Key Points

Qadhf (قَذف) means hurling something to a distant point, and Mala' al-A'la (ملأ اعلى) refers to the exalted station of the angels. Duhūran (دحوراً) means driving away forcefully, with humiliation and disgrace. Wāsib (واصب) means permanent (or lasting). Khafṭah (خَطفة) means snatching quickly. Thāqib (ثاقب) means penetrating and piercing. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله says: On the Night of Ascension (Mi'raj), I saw an angel who was tasked with pursuing those who steal news (of the heavens), and seventy thousand angels were under his command, each of whom commanded seventy thousand other angels.(4)

4) Tafasir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Burhan.

Messages

1- The information and news concerning the inhabitants of the earth are available to the angels of the high realm, where they converse with one another, and the devils can hear their conversation. «لا یسّمّعون الى الملأ الاعلى و یقذفون...»

2- Eavesdropping (stealing a hearing) is a satanic act and must be countered. «حفظاً من كل شیطان مارد لا یسّمّعون»

3- The army of Allah is present everywhere. «یقذفون من كل جانب»

4- The devils (or evil elements) in society must be dealt with decisively. «یقذفون من كل جانب»

5- Devils are accountable beings subject to punishment and reckoning by Allah. «و لهم عذاب واصب»

6- Insist upon the preservation of secrets. «حفظاً... یقذفون... دحوراً... فاتبعه شهاب»

7- Gaining knowledge of the secrets of the high realm is so important to the devils that even though they are targeted from every side, they still attempt to eavesdrop stealthily. «الا من خطف الخطفة»

8- Meteors and celestial stones are divine agents that are hurled with purpose, not randomly or aimlessly. «فاتبعه شهاب ثاقب»

9- The devils are destroyed before they can utilize the unseen news of the heavens. «ثاقب»

10- Devils are visible and targetable beings. «فاتبعه شهاب ثاقب»

Ayahs

فَٱسْتَفْتِهِمْ أَهُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقًا أَم مَّنْ خَلَقْنَآ ۚ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰهُم مِّن طِينٍ لَّازِبٍۭ (۱۱)
Ask them, is their creation more prodigious or [that of other creatures] that We have created? Indeed, We created them from a viscous clay. (11)
بَلْ عَجِبْتَ وَيَسْخَرُونَ (۱۲)
Indeed you wonder, while they engage in ridicule, (12)
وَإِذَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ لَا يَذْكُرُونَ (۱۳)
and [even] when admonished do not take admonition, (13)
وَإِذَا رَأَوْا۟ ءَايَةً يَسْتَسْخِرُونَ (۱٤)
and when they see a sign they make it an object of ridicule, (14)
وَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱٥)
and say, ‘This is nothing but plain magic!’ (15)

Messages

1- Comparison and questioning is a method for prompting individuals to reflect. «فاستفتهم» (How does the creation of man compare to the protection of the vast heavens?)

2- Temper proud and mocking individuals by reminding them of the origin of their own creation. «طین لازب»

3- The counsel of the noblest educators is ineffective for hard-hearted individuals. «ذكّروا لایذكرون»

4- Being corrupt provides the basis for leading others toward corruption. «یستسخرون»

5- Sometimes the enemy resorts to mockery instead of accepting truth and logic. «یستسخرون»

6- Sometimes the enemy attempts to speak with insistence and certainty in their propaganda. «ان هذا الا سحرٌ مبین»

7- Opponents acknowledge the extraordinary impact of the Quran. (The polytheists considered the Quran magic, and magic refers to an extraordinary deed.)

8- Mockery and treating sacred things lightly is the prelude to disbelief and the denial of realities. «یستسخرون و قالوا... سحرٌ مبین»

Ayahs

أَءِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا وَعِظَـٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ (۱٦)
‘What! When we are dead and have become dust and bones, shall we be resurrected? (16)
أَوَءَابَآؤُنَا ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ (۱۷)
And our forefathers too?!’ (17)
قُلْ نَعَمْ وَأَنتُمْ دَٰخِرُونَ (۱۸)
Say, ‘Yes! And you will be utterly humble.’ (18)
فَإِنَّمَا هِىَ زَجْرَةٌ وَٰحِدَةٌ فَإِذَا هُمْ يَنظُرُونَ (۱۹)
It will be only a single shout and, behold, they will look on, (19)

Key Points

«داخر» means humiliation and abasement, and «زجرة» means a great cry or shout. Those with sick hearts strive to spread doubts. Although one question would have sufficed, they posed three consecutive questions: «ءاذا - ءانّا - أو آباءنا».

Messages

1- Deniers of the Resurrection lack sound logic and reasoning; instead of providing arguments, they merely express incredulity (finding it far-fetched). «ءاذا...»

2- The polytheists considered the resurrection of their ancestors more unlikely than their own resurrection. «ءانّا... أو آباؤنا»

3- Questions and doubts, even if accompanied by obstinacy and ill-intent, must be answered explicitly. «قل نعم»

4- Man turning into dust is not an obstacle to his being brought back to life. «قل نعم»

5- Obstinate denial of the Resurrection leads to humiliation on the Day of Judgment. «و انتم داخرون»

6- The advent of the Resurrection will be with a sound and a cry. «فانما هى زجرة»

7- The advent of the Resurrection is sudden, not gradual. «زجرة واحدة»

8- Bringing about the Resurrection is easy for Allah. «فانّما هى زجرة واحدة»

9- On the Day of Judgment, the polytheists will look upon their wretched state in astonishment. «فاذاهم ینظرون»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰوَيْلَنَا هَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ (۲٠)
and say, ‘Woe to us! This is the Day of Retribution!’ (20)
هَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلْفَصْلِ ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ (۲۱)
‘This is the Day of Judgement that you used to deny!’ (21)
۞ ٱحْشُرُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ وَأَزْوَٰجَهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْبُدُونَ (۲۲)
‘Muster the wrongdoers and their mates and what they used to worship (22)
مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَٱهْدُوهُمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۲۳)
besides Allah, and show them the way to hell! (23)
وَقِفُوهُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُم مَّسْـُٔولُونَ (۲٤)
[But first] stop them! For they must be questioned.’ (24)

Key Points

Azwāj (أزواج) can mean both "those who share the same ideas/thoughts" (like-minded people) and "polytheist spouses." It is mentioned in both Shi'a and Sunni narrations that among the instances of questioning referred to in the Ayah, "وقفوهم انّهم مسئولون" (And stop them; indeed, they are to be questioned), is the questioning about the Wilāyah (guardianship) and leadership of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام. (5) Narrations state that several things will be questioned on the Day of Judgment: the leadership of the infallible Imam علیهم السلام, one's lifespan and youth, income, expenditure and consumption, attending to the poor, and innovations (in religion/heresies). (6)

5) Tafsir Namūneh. 6) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 134; Wasā'il, Vol. 4, p. 124.

Messages

1- The denial of the Resurrection will be the cause of the disbelievers' regret. "یا ویلنا" (Woe to us!)

2- The Day of Judgment is the day of regret and admission for the deniers. "یا ویلنا..." But these regrets will be of no benefit, and the command to guide them to Hell will be issued. "فاهدوهم"

3- On the Day of Judgment, in addition to Hellfire, there will be humiliation, criticism, and psychological torment. "هذا یوم الفصل الّذى كنتم به تكذّبون" (This is the Day of Separation which you used to deny.) (In this very section, we also read: "فاهدوهم الى صراط الجحیم" [Then guide them to the path of the Hellfire], even though the word hidāyah (guidance) is used for leading to good deeds, it is used here for Hell as a form of mockery and humiliation.)

4- The disbelievers look toward the retribution, "یوم الدین" (Day of Recompense), but Allah looks toward the judgment. "هذا یوم الفصل" (This is the Day of Separation/Decision).

5- Worse than denying the truth is the continuation (persistence) of denial. "كنتم به تكذبون" (You used to deny it).

6- Denying the Resurrection is oppression against oneself. "تكذبون... الّذین ظلموا..." (You deny... those who oppressed...). Just as polytheism (shirk) is a great oppression. (Indeed, the greatest characteristic of the inhabitants of Hell is oppression/injustice.)

7- The angels are involved in gathering and sending the polytheists to Hell. The angels are addressed: "احشروا الّذین ظلموا" (Gather those who oppressed).

8- Everyone will be gathered and accompanied by their like-minded associates and beloved ones. "ظلموا و ازواجهم و ما كانوا یعبدون" (Those who oppressed, and their associates, and what they used to worship).

9- False deities are not only incapable of solving problems but are themselves afflicted (or trapped). "و ما كانوا یعبدون" (And what they used to worship).

10- It is sufficient for a person's wretchedness that he be gathered with lifeless idols. "احشروا... و ما كانوا یعبدون" (Gather... and what they used to worship).

11- Whoever does not accept Divine guidance in this world will be guided to Hell in the Hereafter. "فاهدوهم" (Then guide them).

12- The end of polytheism (shirk) and disbelief (kufr) is Hell. "فاهدوهم الى صراط الجحیم" (Then guide them to the path of the Hellfire).

13- Although the end and destiny of the deniers of the Resurrection is Hell, "فاهدوهم الى صراط الجحیم" (Then guide them to the path of the Hellfire), they will nonetheless be questioned about blessings and duties. "مسئولون" (They are to be questioned).

Ayahs

مَا لَكُمْ لَا تَنَاصَرُونَ (۲٥)
‘Why is it that you do not support one another [today]?’ (25)
بَلْ هُمُ ٱلْيَوْمَ مُسْتَسْلِمُونَ (۲٦)
‘Indeed, they are [meek and] submissive today!’ (26)
وَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (۲۷)
Some of them will turn to others, questioning each other. (27)
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّكُمْ كُنتُمْ تَأْتُونَنَا عَنِ ٱلْيَمِينِ (۲۸)
They will say, ‘Indeed you used to accost us peremptorily.’ (28)
قَالُوا۟ بَل لَّمْ تَكُونُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ (۲۹)
They will answer, ‘No, you [yourselves] had no faith. (29)

Key Points

The term «مستسلمون» (mustaslimūn) is derived from the Istif'āl form. It either signifies exaggeration in submission, meaning complete surrender, or it means that instead of supporting each other, the polytheists surrender one another to the Divine wrath. «یمین» (yamīn) is either derived from «یُمن» (yumn), meaning goodness and blessing—implying that "you approached us under the guise of goodwill and led us astray"—or it is a metaphor for power and might, as the right hand is a symbol of strength. In numerous verses of the Quran, dialogues are mentioned between the inhabitants of Hell with one another, with Satan, with the believers, with the angels, and with Allah, in which various pleas for deliverance are raised. However, in all cases, they receive a negative response. In some instances, they shift the blame onto one another, saying: "Had it not been for you, we would certainly have been believers" «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین». But they are answered that this is not the case; rather, you yourselves were not believers: «بل لم تكونوا مؤمنین».

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, criminals will not be able to offer any help or assistance to one another. «ما لكم لا تناصرون»

2- The arrogant will surrender on the Day of Judgment. «بل هم الیوم مستسلمون»

3- On the Day of Judgment, criminals will exonerate themselves and shift their guilt onto others. «تأتوننا عن الیمین»

4- The use of power (7) or the pretense of goodwill (8) are methods employed by the leaders of disbelief and polytheism to deceive others. «تأتوننا عن الیمین»

5- On the Day of Judgment, no one will be willing to bear the guilt of others. «لم تكونوا مؤمنین»

7) If we take the word «یمین» (yamīn) as a sign of the right hand and power.

8) If we consider the word «یمین» (yamīn) to be derived from «یُمن» (yumn), meaning goodness and blessing.

Ayahs

وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا عَلَيْكُم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍۭ ۖ بَلْ كُنتُمْ قَوْمًا طَـٰغِينَ (۳٠)
We had no authority over you. No, you [yourselves] were a rebellious lot. (30)
فَحَقَّ عَلَيْنَا قَوْلُ رَبِّنَآ ۖ إِنَّا لَذَآئِقُونَ (۳۱)
So our Lord’s word became due against us that we shall indeed taste [the punishment]. (31)
فَأَغْوَيْنَـٰكُمْ إِنَّا كُنَّا غَـٰوِينَ (۳۲)
So we perverted you, for we were perverse [ourselves].’ (32)
فَإِنَّهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ فِى ٱلْعَذَابِ مُشْتَرِكُونَ (۳۳)
So, that day they will share the punishment. (33)

Messages

1- The leaders of disbelief confess both to their own misguidance and accept the responsibility for deceiving and misleading their followers. «اغویناكم» But they do not accept the responsibility for coercion, dominance, or authority. «ما كان لنا علیكم من سلطان»

2- The basis and cause of deviation originate from within the deviants, not as a result of external pressure. «كنتم قوماً طاغین»

3- Ultimately, the leaders of polytheism (shirk) will confess to Divine Monotheism (Tawhid) and Lordship (Rububiyyah) on the Day of Judgment. «قول ربّنا»

4- In this world, the promise and threat of Allah had reached the ears of the leaders of polytheism, and they denied it intentionally. «قول ربّنا»

5- Man is a being free from social and cultural determinism. «بل كنتم قوماً طاغین»

6- Being misguided provides the basis for misleading others. «اغویناكم انا كنا غاوین»

7- Allah is just in the punishment of the leaders and followers of disbelief. «فى العذاب مشتركون»

8- Ordinary people are influential both in the emergence of corrupt systems or individuals and in the growth, strengthening, and continuation of their oppression. For if they had no share or effect, they would not be partners in the punishment. «فى العذاب مشتركون»

Ayahs

إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَفْعَلُ بِٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۳٤)
Indeed, that is how We deal with the guilty. (34)
إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوٓا۟ إِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (۳٥)
Indeed, it was they who, when they were told, ‘There is no god except Allah,’ used to be disdainful, (35)
وَيَقُولُونَ أَئِنَّا لَتَارِكُوٓا۟ ءَالِهَتِنَا لِشَاعِرٍ مَّجْنُونٍۭ (۳٦)
and [they would] say, ‘Shall we abandon our gods for a crazy poet?’ (36)
بَلْ جَآءَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَصَدَّقَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۳۷)
Indeed, he has brought [them] the truth, and confirmed the [earlier] apostles. (37)
إِنَّكُمْ لَذَآئِقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَلِيمِ (۳۸)
Indeed you will taste the painful punishment, (38)

Messages

1- The tradition (Sunnah) of Divine Justice regarding the punishment of criminals is uniform. «انّا كذلك نفعل بالمجرمین»

2- The root of the torment (Azaab) is the crime committed by humans. «نفعل بالمجرمین»

3- The sign of a criminal is possessing an arrogant spirit (Istikbar) in the face of Monotheism (Tawhid). «یستكبرون»

4- The Prophets called people towards Allah, not towards themselves. «قیل لهم لا اله الا اللّه»

5- Slander, belittling others, and fanaticism regarding superstitious beliefs are signs of arrogance (Istikbar). «یستكبرون و یقولون... لشاعر مجنون»

6- Arrogance (Takabbur) overturns human thought. (It makes them consider the wisest of people insane and stone idols as gods). «الهتنا لشاعر مجنون»

7- Slanders must be answered. «بل جاء بالحقّ»

8- The words of the Prophet of Islam and the slogan لا اله الا اللّه created a strong attraction in human nature (Fitrah). (The accusation of being a poet was due to the attractiveness of the Prophet's words). «لشاعر»

9- The polytheists (Mushrikin) had multiple objects of worship. «آلهتنا»

10- The teachings of all Prophets are one; all Prophets invited to Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Truth (Haqq), and the Prophet of Islam also confirmed them. «و صدّق المرسلین»

11- Be people of the Truth yourselves, and also confirm the truthfulness of others. «جاء بالحقّ و صدّق المرسلین»

12- The punishment for those who knowingly called the Quran poetry and the Prophet insane is painful torment. «العذاب الالیم»

Ayahs

وَمَا تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۳۹)
and you will be requited only for what you used to do (39)
إِلَّا عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (٤٠)
—[all] except Allah’s exclusive servants. (40)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ رِزْقٌ مَّعْلُومٌ (٤۱)
For such there is a known provision (41)
فَوَٰكِهُ ۖ وَهُم مُّكْرَمُونَ (٤۲)
—fruits—and they will be held in honour, (42)
فِى جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (٤۳)
in the gardens of bliss, (43)
عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مُّتَقَـٰبِلِينَ (٤٤)
[reclining] on couches, facing one another, (44)

Key Points

Mukhliṣ (مخلِص) refers to individuals who avoid all types of polytheism (shirk), hypocrisy (riyā), and deceit (nifāq), and dedicate themselves as servants of Allah. However, Mukhlaṣ (مخلَص) refers to individuals whom Allah has purified and chosen due to the perfections they possess.

Messages

1- Divine punishments are just. «و ما تجزون الا ما كنتم تعملون» (Indeed, the painful torment of the Day of Judgment is the embodiment of the same polytheistic and arrogant behavior [they exhibited] in the world.)

2- Alongside threat, good tidings are necessary. «الاّ عباد اللّه المخلصین»

3- What causes man to be chosen by Allah is servitude (to Him). «الاّ عباداللّه المخلَصین»

4- A proportionate and equal reward is for the transgressors, but the reward of Allah’s chosen servants is beyond their performance. «و ما تجزون الا ما كنتم تعملون الاّ عباداللّه»

5- In Paradise, material and spiritual enjoyment exist side by side. «فواكه... مكرمون»

6- Meeting the friends (Awliyā) of Allah is one of the spiritual pleasures of Paradise. «سُرر متقابلین»

7- In worldly confrontations, there is sometimes enmity, but in the confrontations of the Hereafter, there is pleasant gathering and enjoyment. «على سرر متقابلین»

8- The enjoyment of the people of Paradise is comprehensive:

A) Being chosen by Allah (Mukhlaṣīn). (Allah’s chosen ones possess a very high status. «اولئك» [those] is used to refer to the distant, indicating their exalted status.)

B) A "known" provision (rizq ma‘lūm), which, according to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, the servants of Paradise know and bring to the friends of Allah before they even request it.(9)

C) The provision being both material and spiritual. «فواكه و هم مكرمون»

D) An excellent abode. «جنات النعیم»

E) Presence among the friends of Paradise, accompanied by intimacy, affection, and pleasant gathering.

9) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

يُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم بِكَأْسٍ مِّن مَّعِينٍۭ (٤٥)
served around with a cup, from a clear fountain, (45)
بَيْضَآءَ لَذَّةٍ لِّلشَّـٰرِبِينَ (٤٦)
snow-white, delicious to the drinkers, (46)
لَا فِيهَا غَوْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ عَنْهَا يُنزَفُونَ (٤۷)
wherein there will be neither headache nor will it cause them stupefaction, (47)
وَعِندَهُمْ قَـٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرْفِ عِينٌ (٤۸)
and with them will be maidens, of restrained glances with big [beautiful] eyes, (48)
كَأَنَّهُنَّ بَيْضٌ مَّكْنُونٌ (٤۹)
as if they were hidden ostrich eggs. (49)

Key Points

«قَدَح» (Qadah) is an empty vessel, which, when filled, is called «كأس» (Ka's). Sometimes the word Ka's is used, but the intention is the wine itself, not the vessel. The word «مَعین» (Ma'īn) refers to clear water that is flowing. «غَول» (Ghawl) means hidden corruption or ruin. «یُنزَفون» (Yunzafūn) means the gradual fading away of the intellect (or reason). «طَرف» (Ṭarf) means the eyelid, and «قاصرات الطرف» (Qāṣirāt aṭ-Ṭarf) is a metaphor indicating that the wives of Paradise lower their gaze from others, or that they look with a half-closed eye, which has a special attraction. Perhaps the intention is that the women of Paradise are so beautiful that they capture the husband's gaze exclusively, meaning that due to their beauty, their husbands look only at them. The word «عین» (ʿĪn) is the plural of «عیناء» (ʿAynā'), meaning large, beautiful, and dark (black) eyes. The word «بیض» (Bīḍ) is the plural of «بیضه» (Bayḍah), meaning an ostrich egg, whose color is white, shining, and slightly yellowish, and which the animal covers under its wing so that it does not become dusty. The Arabs liken beautiful women to it. (10)

10) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- Physical pleasures related to the stomach and desire (appetite) will also exist in the Hereafter. «فواكه, كأس من معین, قاصرات الطرف»

2- Enjoyment in Paradise is not limited to a specific direction; rather, blessings are circulated around the people of Paradise from every side. «یُطاف»

3- White is one of the colors of Paradise, signifying brightness, beauty, purity, and a pleasing color. «بیضاء , بیض»

4- The pleasures of Paradise are free from hidden and manifest side effects. The wine of Paradise is delicious, but it does not have the intoxication or other side effects of worldly wine. «لذّة للشاربین لا فیها غول...»

5- Restraining the gaze from others is one of the characteristics of the women of Paradise. «قاصرات الطرف»

6- The women of Paradise are both beautiful and captivating, and also pure, chaste, and protected (covered). «عین, بیض مكنون»

Ayahs

فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (٥٠)
Some of them will turn to others, questioning each other. (50)
قَالَ قَآئِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ إِنِّى كَانَ لِى قَرِينٌ (٥۱)
One of them will say, ‘Indeed I had a companion (51)
يَقُولُ أَءِنَّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُصَدِّقِينَ (٥۲)
who used to say, ‘‘Are you really among those who affirm (52)
أَءِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا وَعِظَـٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَدِينُونَ (٥۳)
that when we have died and become dust and bones, we shall be brought to retribution?’’ ’ (53)

Key Points

One of the names of the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah) is "Yawm ad-Dīn" (Day of Judgment), and madīnūn (the judged/indebted) comes from dīn, meaning recompense (Jazā').

Messages

  1. Memories of the world are not forgotten in Paradise. «كان لى قرین»
  2. In the gatherings of Paradise, where they are seated on couches facing one another, they engage in questioning and conversation about their worldly companions. «كان لى قرین»
  3. Companionship in the world with disbelievers (kuffār) is permissible, provided one maintains their religious beliefs. «كان لى قرین»
  4. Recounting the denials and reproaches of opponents is part of the conversations of the people of Paradise. «فاقبل بعضهم على بعض یتسائلون... یقول ءانّك لمن المصدّقین»
  5. The disbelievers (kuffār) have no proof whatsoever for the non-existence of the Resurrection (Ma'ād); what they possess is merely incredulity and astonishment. «ءاذا متنا و كنّا تراباً...»

Ayahs

قَالَ هَلْ أَنتُم مُّطَّلِعُونَ (٥٤)
He will say, ‘Will you have a look?’ (54)
فَٱطَّلَعَ فَرَءَاهُ فِى سَوَآءِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (٥٥)
Then he will take a look and sight him in the middle of hell. (55)
قَالَ تَٱللَّهِ إِن كِدتَّ لَتُرْدِينِ (٥٦)
He will say, ‘By Allah, you had almost ruined me! (56)
وَلَوْلَا نِعْمَةُ رَبِّى لَكُنتُ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْضَرِينَ (٥۷)
Had it not been for my Lord’s blessing, I too would have been among those mustered [in hell]!’ (57)

Key Points

The word turdīn (تُردین) is derived from irdā’ (ارداء), meaning a fall from a height that is accompanied by destruction.

Messages

1- The inhabitants of Paradise become informed about the state of the inhabitants of Hell. (Fāṭṭala‘)

2- Deniers of the Resurrection are in the middle of Hell. (Sawā’ al-Jaḥīm)

3- The inhabitants of Paradise can converse with their friends who are in Hell. (Qāl...)

4- We must be wary of the danger posed by bad friends. (Kitta laturdīn)

5- Corrupt individuals strive to corrupt others. (Kitta laturdīn)

6- If Allah’s grace were to cease for a single moment, the fall of man would be certain. (Lawlā ni‘matu Rabbī lakuntu...)

7- Some sincere servants may go right up to the edge of the abyss, but the grace of Allah saves them. (Lawlā ni‘matu Rabbī)

8- Not being influenced by corrupt friends is a divine blessing. (Lawlā ni‘matu Rabbī)

9- The inhabitants of Paradise do not consider themselves deserving; rather, they consider their entry into Paradise to be indebted to the grace and blessing of Allah. (Lawlā ni‘matu Rabbī lakuntu min al-muḥḍarīn)

Ayahs

أَفَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَيِّتِينَ (٥۸)
‘Is it [true] that we shall not die [anymore], (58)
إِلَّا مَوْتَتَنَا ٱلْأُولَىٰ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ (٥۹)
aside from our earlier death, and that we shall not be punished? (59)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (٦٠)
This is indeed the supreme triumph!’ (60)
لِمِثْلِ هَـٰذَا فَلْيَعْمَلِ ٱلْعَـٰمِلُونَ (٦۱)
Let all workers work for the like of this! (61)

Messages

1- The inhabitants of Paradise are so joyful that it is as if they cannot believe the blessings of Paradise and they express astonishment at the eternity of these blessings. «أَفَمَا نَحْنُ بِمَيِّتِينَ»

2- Death and annihilation have no place in Paradise. «إِلَّا مَوْتَتَنَا الْأُولَىٰ» This is contrary to Hell, where criminals die due to the torment and are then brought back to life.

3- Sinful believers will first go to Hell and then to Paradise, because torment after Paradise makes no sense. «وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ»

4- For a person to remain sound (in faith) in corrupt environments is possible but difficult. «إِنَّ هَذَا لَهُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ»

5- Salvation from bad friends in this world and from the torment of Hell in the Hereafter is the great success. «لَهُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ»

6- We should introduce proper role models to the people. «لِمِثْلِ هَذَا»

7- Working to attain the blessings of Paradise does not contradict sincerity (Ikhlas). «لِمِثْلِ هَذَا فَلْيَعْمَلِ»

8- Human actions in this world must be aimed at achieving a lofty goal and great success; otherwise, they result in regret and loss. «لِمِثْلِ هَذَا فَلْيَعْمَلِ»

9- Faith alone is not sufficient; action is necessary. «فَلْيَعْمَلِ الْعَامِلُونَ»

Ayahs

أَذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ نُّزُلًا أَمْ شَجَرَةُ ٱلزَّقُّومِ (٦۲)
Is this a better reception, or the Zaqqum tree? (62)
إِنَّا جَعَلْنَـٰهَا فِتْنَةً لِّلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٦۳)
Indeed We have made it a punishment for the wrongdoers. (63)
إِنَّهَا شَجَرَةٌ تَخْرُجُ فِىٓ أَصْلِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (٦٤)
It is a tree that rises from the depths of hell. (64)

Key Points

Nuzul refers to the hospitality offered to a guest upon his arrival and entry. Zaqqūm is a poisonous plant that grows in the deserts (badlands). If its leaf is plucked, a sap emerges from that spot which causes swelling wherever it touches the body. In Sūrat ad-Dukhān, Ayah 43, He says: «انّ شجرة الزّقوم طعام الاثیم كالمهل یغلى فى البطون كغلى الحمیم» Zaqqūm is the food of the sinners, which boils inside them like molten metal, similar to the boiling of hot water.

Messages

1- We should compare the examples of Divine grace and Divine wrath with one another so that we may act better. «فلیعمل العاملون، ءذلك خیر... ام شجرة الزقّوم»

2- Man is inherently seeking goodness and pursuing the best things. «ذلك خیر... ام»

3- The inhabitants of Paradise are the guests of Allah. «نُزلاً»

4- The oppressors who burn hearts in this world will be hosted in the Hereafter with burning food. «فتنة للظالمین»

5- By Divine Will, a tree grows in the depths of the Fire. «شجرة تخرج فى اصل الجحیم»

Ayahs

طَلْعُهَا كَأَنَّهُۥ رُءُوسُ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينِ (٦٥)
Its spathes are as if they were devils’ heads. (65)
فَإِنَّهُمْ لَـَٔاكِلُونَ مِنْهَا فَمَالِـُٔونَ مِنْهَا ٱلْبُطُونَ (٦٦)
They will eat from it and gorge with it their bellies. (66)
ثُمَّ إِنَّ لَهُمْ عَلَيْهَا لَشَوْبًا مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ (٦۷)
On top of that they will take a solution of scalding water. (67)
ثُمَّ إِنَّ مَرْجِعَهُمْ لَإِلَى ٱلْجَحِيمِ (٦۸)
Then their retreat will be toward hell. (68)

Key Points

The word ṭal‘ (طلع) means the date palm blossom, as if it is the time of the fruit's emergence (ṭulū‘). The word shawb (شوب) means mixed or blended. A drink consumed after a meal is called shawb because it mixes with the food in the stomach. Since Shayṭān (شیطان - Satan) in Muslim culture refers to an ugly, evil-natured, and malicious being, this Ayah states that the blossoms of the Zaqqūm tree, which ought to be beautiful and captivating, are like the heads of devils (Shayāṭīn). Just as in popular culture, the angel (malak) is the manifestation of goodness and nobility, to the extent that the women of Egypt said in praise of Joseph: «ان هذا الا مَلك كریم» (11)

11) Yūsuf, 31.

Messages

1- The inhabitants of Hell, like the inhabitants of Paradise, have food and drink, but it is not only unappetizing and unpleasant but also ugly, foul-tasting, and painful. «كانه رؤوس الشیاطین... لشوباً من حمیم»

2- There is neither a way to escape nor a way to evade the food of Hell. «اكلون... مالئون»

3- The inhabitants of Hell are so hungry that they fill their stomachs with the worst kind of food. «فمالئون منها البطون»

4- The punishments of the Day of Judgment are not temporary; rather, Hell is the eternal abode. «ثمّ انّ مرجعهم لالى الجحیم» (Perhaps the inhabitants of Hell are taken somewhere to be fed Zaqqūm and Ḥamīm and then returned to their original place.)

Ayahs

إِنَّهُمْ أَلْفَوْا۟ ءَابَآءَهُمْ ضَآلِّينَ (٦۹)
They had found their fathers astray, (69)
فَهُمْ عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِمْ يُهْرَعُونَ (۷٠)
yet they press onwards in their footsteps. (70)
وَلَقَدْ ضَلَّ قَبْلَهُمْ أَكْثَرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۷۱)
Most of the former peoples went astray before them, (71)
وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا فِيهِم مُّنذِرِينَ (۷۲)
and We had certainly sent warners among them. (72)
فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (۷۳)
So observe how was the fate of those who were warned (73)
إِلَّا عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (۷٤)
—[all] except Allah’s exclusive servants! (74)

Key Points

The word «یُهرعون» means to be rushed or hurried. In this Ayah, Allah criticizes those who, without reflection and with haste, follow the ideas of their ancestors. One of the characteristics of the inhabitants of Hell is blind imitation of fathers and forefathers, whereas imitation (taqlid) is not permissible in matters of belief (aqidah); rather, human beliefs must be founded upon reasoning and intellect.

Messages

1- Blind imitation and following ancestors in incorrect thoughts and actions, under whatever title, is unacceptable. «الفوا آبائهم ضالّین... على آثارهم یهرعون»

2- The worst type of imitation is when we imitate the misguided while having knowledge and awareness of their deviation. «الفوا آبائهم ضالّین»

3- The false beliefs of ancestors influence future generations. «آبائهم ضالّین»

4- Losing oneself to the false customs and traditions of ancestors is forbidden. («یُهرعون» means to be forcibly and involuntarily dragged.)

5- The cultural heritage of ancestors is not always and everywhere praiseworthy. «على آثارهم یهرعون»

6- In an environment of deviation, the most important duty is warning. Allah sent warners among the misguided nations. «ضلّ قبلهم... ارسلنا فیهم منذرین»

7- Allah establishes the conclusive proof (Itmām al-Hujjah). «ارسلنا فیهم منذرین»

8- The corrupt majority of a society is not a reason to abandon individuals who are receptive to guidance. «ضل قبلهم اكثر الاولین... ارسلنا فیهم»

9- We should study history that leads to growth and serves as a lesson. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة المنذرین» We are commanded to reflect and ponder the fate of stubborn and prejudiced disbelievers.

10- Despite a corrupt majority, it is possible to be a servant of Allah and not be absorbed into the corrupt society. «الاّ عباد اللّه»

11- Worshipping Allah leads to receiving special favors. «عباداللّه المخلَصین»

12- Sincerity (Ikhlās) in worship is the means of salvation from a bad end. «كیف كان عاقبة المنذرین الا عباد اللّه المخلصین»

13- Only the sincere servants of Allah take the warnings of the Prophets seriously. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة المنذرین الاّ عباداللّه المخلصین»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ نَادَىٰنَا نُوحٌ فَلَنِعْمَ ٱلْمُجِيبُونَ (۷٥)
Certainly Noah called out to Us, and how well did We respond! (75)
وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْكَرْبِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۷٦)
We delivered him and his family from their great distress, (76)
وَجَعَلْنَا ذُرِّيَّتَهُۥ هُمُ ٱلْبَاقِينَ (۷۷)
and made his descendants the survivors, (77)
وَتَرَكْنَا عَلَيْهِ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۷۸)
and left for him a good name among posterity: (78)
سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَىٰ نُوحٍ فِى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۷۹)
‘Peace to Noah, throughout the nations!’ (79)
إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۸٠)
Thus do We reward the virtuous. (80)
إِنَّهُۥ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۸۱)
He is indeed one of Our faithful servants. (81)
ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا ٱلْـَٔاخَرِينَ (۸۲)
Then We drowned the rest. (82)

Key Points

In the Quran, the story of Prophet Noah (حضرت نوح), who is a model and exemplar of steadfastness, is mentioned repeatedly, and his history provided encouragement and tranquility for the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله). This Divine Prophet had several characteristics, including: A) He is the first Prophet of Resolve (Ulul-'Azm) and is named the Elder of the Prophets (Shaykh al-Anbiya). B) He is the only Prophet whose duration of prophethood is mentioned in the Quran. C) Allah's salutation (peace) upon Noah has a special characteristic, as the phrase "فِي الْعَالَمِينَ" (among all peoples) is added to it. D) He is a Prophet whose own son and wife opposed him and did not believe in him.

The word ākhir (آخِر) in Arabic means 'end' and ākhirīn (آخِرین) means 'those who come later' or 'posterity.' However, ākhar (آخَر) means 'other' and ākharīn (آخَرین) means 'the others.'

The difference between the history narrated by the Quran and other history books is that the Quran, after recounting a real event, explains the Divine traditions (Sunan) concerning it. "كَذَلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ" (Thus do We reward the good-doers).

Perhaps the intent of Prophet Noah's call (nidā') in this verse is the same as verse 26 of Surah Al-Mu'minun, where he said: "رَبِّ انصُرْنِي بِمَا كَذَّبُونِ" (My Lord, help me against their denial).

Allah is "نِعْمَ الْمُجِيبُونَ" (the Best of Responders), meaning He answers what is beneficial for the servant, not necessarily what the servant has specified. Therefore, sometimes you have a request, and Allah substitutes it with something else. Sometimes He answers your request with a delay, and sometimes He does not grant your request at all, just as Noah asked for the salvation of his son, but it was not granted.

The intent of the great distress and grief ("الْكَرْبِ الْعَظِيمِ") is either the flood and drowning, or the persecution by the people. Yes, the mission and propagation of religion entail difficulties and sorrows.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The intent of survival (baqā') in the verse "وَجَعَلْنَا ذُرِّيَّتَهُ هُمُ الْبَاقِينَ" (And We made his descendants the ones who remained) is the survival of the religion, the Book, and the prophethood among the progeny and descendants of Noah. (12)

(12) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Men of God do not reach a dead end, and when matters become difficult for them, they solve their problem through prayer and seeking aid from the abundant power of Allah. "وَنَادَانَا نُوحٌ" (And Noah called upon Us).
  2. Although Allah is aware of the inner thoughts of everyone, speaking, requesting, and praying have specific effects. "وَنَادَانَا" (And he called upon Us). (Yes, calling, praying, and expressing need before Allah leads to Divine response).
  3. If someone calls upon God out of sincerity, his prayer will be answered. "وَنَادَانَا... فَلَنِعْمَ الْمُجِيبُونَ" (And he called upon Us... then how excellent are the Responders).
  4. The answering of prayer is accompanied by wisdom and mercy, and is without obligation (or reproach). "نِعْمَ الْمُجِيبُونَ" (How excellent are the Responders).
  5. Noah requested the destruction of the disbelievers and safe passage in the ark for himself and his companions from Allah, but God bestowed upon him a good reputation, the survival of his lineage, and His eternal peace. Perhaps this is the meaning of "نِعْمَ الْمُجِيبُونَ," meaning, 'We gave more than you asked for.'
  6. Allah preserved Noah in the water and Abraham in the fire. "نَجَّيْنَاهُ" (We saved him). (Water and fire are nothing against the Will of God).
  7. Kinship and lineage are not always the cause of being considered 'family' (ahl). (This verse states: We saved the family of Noah, while we know that Allah drowned his son. Therefore, the intent of 'family' (ahl) is his like-minded followers, not merely his relatives). "أَهْلَهُ" (his family).
  8. The extinction or survival of every generation is in the Hand of God. "وَجَعَلْنَا ذُرِّيَّتَهُ هُمُ الْبَاقِينَ" (And We made his descendants the ones who remained).
  9. The human race today descends from Prophet Noah and his companions. "هُمُ الْبَاقِينَ" (the ones who remained).
  10. The friends of God (Awliyā') are alive and receive salutations. "سَلَامٌ عَلَى نُوحٍ فِي الْعَالَمِينَ" (Peace be upon Noah among all peoples).
  11. The factor for the survival of a good reputation among people and peace from Allah is good-doing (righteousness). "نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ" (We reward the good-doers).
  12. The Divine reward for the good-doers is a continuous process and tradition (Sunnah), not an accident or a spark. "كَذَلِكَ نَجْزِي" (Thus do We reward).
  13. Allah also gives the reward of the good-doers in this world. "إِنَّا كَذَلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ" (Indeed, thus do We reward the good-doers).
  14. Sending peace upon the Prophets and Awliyā' of the past is a Divine act. "سَلَامٌ عَلَى نُوحٍ فِي الْعَالَمِينَ" (Peace be upon Noah among all peoples).
  15. Good-doers attain Divine ranks when their deeds stem from faith, not for fame or reputation. "نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ" (We reward the good-doers. Indeed, he was one of Our believing servants).
  16. If benevolence and good-doing are accompanied by faith and servitude, they hold greater value with Allah. "إِنَّا كَذَلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ إِنَّهُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا الْمُؤْمِنِينَ" (Indeed, thus do We reward the good-doers. Indeed, he was one of Our believing servants).
  17. When punishment descends in this world, only the believers are saved, and others are punished, whether they are disbelievers or indifferent. The verse says: "وَأَغْرَقْنَا الْآخَرِينَ" (And We drowned the others), and does not say: "We drowned the disbelievers."

Ayahs

۞ وَإِنَّ مِن شِيعَتِهِۦ لَإِبْرَٰهِيمَ (۸۳)
Indeed Abraham was among his followers, (83)
إِذْ جَآءَ رَبَّهُۥ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ (۸٤)
when he came to his Lord with a sound heart [untainted by sin]. (84)
إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ مَاذَا تَعْبُدُونَ (۸٥)
When he said to his father and his people, ‘What is it that you are worshiping? (85)
أَئِفْكًا ءَالِهَةً دُونَ ٱللَّهِ تُرِيدُونَ (۸٦)
Is it a lie, gods other than Allah, that you desire? (86)
فَمَا ظَنُّكُم بِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۸۷)
Then what is your idea about the Lord of all the worlds?’ (87)

Key Points

The word "Shi'a" (شيعة) means a group that follows its leader. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: A sound heart (Qalb Salim) is a heart that is not attached to anything except Allah, the Exalted.(13) Indeed, polytheism (Shirk) is one of the diseases of the heart. Prophet Abraham had special characteristics among the prophets, including: A) Although he was one person, the Quran considers him an entire nation (Ummah). «انّ ابراهیم كان امّة واحدة»(14) B) In addition to prophethood (Nubuwwah), he became the Imam (leader) of the people. «انّى جاعلك للناس اماما»(15) C) Prophets such as Moses, Jesus, and Muhammad علیهم السلام are from his progeny. D) The rituals of Hajj are among his legacies. E) He attained knowledge of the dominion (Malakut) and the inner reality of the heavens. «نرى ابراهیم ملكوت السموات والارض»(16) F) Special titles are mentioned for him in the Quran, such as: Siddiq (Truthful), Awwah (Oft-turning to God), Halim (Forbearing), Khalil (Friend), Hanif (Upright), Qanit (Devout), and possessor of a sound heart (Qalb Salim).

13) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 14) An-Nahl, 120. 15) Al-Baqarah, 124. 16) Al-An'am, 75.

Messages

1- Distance is not a factor in the spiritual journey. (Although the time gap between Prophet Abraham and Prophet Noah is said to be several thousand years, the Quran introduces Abraham as a Shi'a and follower of Noah, and Noah as the pioneer of the monotheistic movement.) «و انّ من شیعته لابراهیم»

2- The path of all prophets is one, and the prophets are role models and leaders for one another; the element of time and place does not affect the fundamentals and principles of the divine religions. «و انّ من شیعته لابراهیم» (In another place, the Quran addresses the Prophet of Islam, saying: «فبهداهم اقتده»(17) Follow their guidance [the guidance of the previous prophets].)

3- Contrary to the claim of the opponents of Shi'ism, the word Shi'a was not invented by the companions of Imam Ali عليه السلام. «و انّ من شیعته»

4- Some of the duties of the prophets are as follows: A) Connection with previous prophets «و انّ من شیعته لابراهیم» B) Submission to Allah «جاء ربّه بقلب سلیم» C) Saving society from all forms of polytheism (Shirk). «اذقال لابیه»

5- First purify your inner self, then proceed to purify society. (The prerequisite for the success of religious leaders is possessing a sound heart.) «جاء ربّه بقلب سلیم اذقال لابیه»

6- A sound heart (Qalb Salim) is a condition for drawing near to Allah and gaining access to His presence. «جاء ربّه بقلب سلیم»

7- The true Shi'a and genuine follower of the prophets is one who possesses a sound and pure heart. «و انّ من شیعته لابراهیم اذ جاء ربّه بقلب سلیم»

8- Frankness, courage, and disregard for tribal or familial considerations are signs of firm faith. Abraham condemned the religion of his father. «قال لابیه و قومه ماذا تعبدون»

9- Men of God are not absorbed into a corrupt environment; rather, they change the environment. «قال لابیه و قومه ماذا تعبدون»

10- In forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), age is not a condition. «لابیه» Number is not a condition. «لابیه و قومه»

11- In forbidding evil, we should start with our own people. «لابیه و قومه»

12- In forbidding evil, we should start with the fundamental evils, such as polytheism (Shirk). «ماذا تعبدون»

13- The forefront of the prophets' struggle is against polytheism (Shirk). «قال لابیه و قومه ماذا تعبدون»

14- In forbidding evil, we should also seek acknowledgment from the conscience of the wrongdoer. «ماذا تعبدون»

15- In forbidding evil, the probability of immediate and complete effect is not a condition; perhaps it will take effect later, perhaps gradually, and perhaps... . «قال... ماذا تعبدون»

16- Idol worship is a type of falsehood. «ائفكاً آلهة»

17- Not only worship, but even the intention to worship other than Allah is ugly. «تعبدون, تریدون»

18- Existence is managed under the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. «بربّ العالمین»

17) Al-An'am, 90.

Ayahs

فَنَظَرَ نَظْرَةً فِى ٱلنُّجُومِ (۸۸)
Then he made an observation of the stars (88)
فَقَالَ إِنِّى سَقِيمٌ (۸۹)
and said, ‘Indeed I am sick!’ (89)
فَتَوَلَّوْا۟ عَنْهُ مُدْبِرِينَ (۹٠)
So they went away leaving him behind. (90)
فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ ءَالِهَتِهِمْ فَقَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ (۹۱)
Then he stole away to their gods and said, ‘Will you not eat? (91)
مَا لَكُمْ لَا تَنطِقُونَ (۹۲)
Why do you not speak?’ (92)

Key Points

The people of Babylon, the place where Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام lived, held a special festival every year. They prepared foods which they placed in the idol temple to be blessed. Then, they collectively went outside the city, and after enjoying themselves, they returned to the idol temple at the end of the day to eat the food. The night before the festival, they also invited Ibrahim to participate in the ceremony with them. However, Prophet Ibrahim, who was waiting for an opportunity to smash the idols and shock the people, looked at the stars—in accordance with the customs and beliefs of the people of Babylon who considered the stars influential in their destiny—and pretended that the position of the celestial bodies indicated a kind of illness if He were to leave the city. Consequently, the people were convinced and stopped insisting. It is clear that Prophet Ibrahim Himself did not hold such a belief regarding the role of the stars, but used a method acceptable to the people themselves to convince them. Just as, in inviting them to monotheism, He first acted as if He had become a star worshipper like them, and then disavowed that belief.(18)

18) An'am, 76 - 78.

Messages

1- In propagation (Da'wah), one must speak to every group in their own language. (For a group that believes in the influence of the stars, one must speak using their perspective and viewpoint.) «فنظر نظرة فى النجوم فقال»

2- To invite people to the path of truth and to combat falsehood, innovative and revolutionary plans are necessary. (Prophet Ibrahim refused to leave the city with the people and carried out His plan, which was smashing the idols.) «فقال انّى سقیم»

3- A believer must be shrewd and sharp-sighted, and utilize opportunities. «فنظر... فقال»

4- The Prophets have a long-term plan from Allah which they execute at the appropriate time. (In Ayah 57 of Surah Al-Anbiya, we read: Ibrahim warned the people that in their absence, He would devise a plot against their idols. «تاللّه لاكیدنّ اصنامكم بعد ان تولّوا مدبرین» That promise was fulfilled through such planning.)

5- Religious leaders in society must act actively, not passively, against deviant intellectual currents, and place the enemy before a fait accompli through a calculated plan. «فنظر... فقال... فراغ»

6- Deceiving the enemy in order to strike him requires knowledge of his customs, traditions, and beliefs. «فنظر... فقال انّى سقیم»

7- The logic of the Prophets for negating idol worship is very clear, innate (fitrī), and rational. «الا تأكلون ما لكم لا تنطقون»

Ayahs

فَرَاغَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضَرْبًۢا بِٱلْيَمِينِ (۹۳)
Then he attacked them, striking forcefully. (93)
فَأَقْبَلُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ يَزِفُّونَ (۹٤)
They came running towards him. (94)
قَالَ أَتَعْبُدُونَ مَا تَنْحِتُونَ (۹٥)
He said, ‘Do you worship what you have yourselves carved, (95)
وَٱللَّهُ خَلَقَكُمْ وَمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۹٦)
when Allah has created you and whatever you make?’ (96)
قَالُوا۟ ٱبْنُوا۟ لَهُۥ بُنْيَـٰنًا فَأَلْقُوهُ فِى ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۹۷)
They said, ‘Build a structure for him and cast him into a huge fire.’ (97)
فَأَرَادُوا۟ بِهِۦ كَيْدًا فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْأَسْفَلِينَ (۹۸)
So they sought to outwit him, but We made them the lowermost. (98)

Key Points

"Rāgha" (راغ) means going towards something away from people's sight. "Yaziffūn" (یزفّون) means moving with speed and haste, and "Zifāf" (زفاف) is the ceremony of taking the bride, accompanied by joy and excitement. Since Abraham (Ibrāhīm) had sworn that he would devise a plan against the idols, "تَاللَّهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصْنَامَكُم" (19) as soon as he saw that reasoning and admonition had no effect, he acted upon that oath and single-handedly smashed all the idols. "فَرَاغَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضَرْبًا بِالْيَمِينِ" Allah also praised Abraham for his faithfulness. "إِبْرَاهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى" (20)

19) Anbiyā, 57. 20) Najm, 37.

Messages

1- Not all plans should be clear and public; sometimes an action must be carried out away from the sight of opponents. "فَرَاغَ"

2- Enjoining the right and forbidding the wrong (Nahy ‘anil-Munkar) has stages: if the first stage, which is dialogue, is ineffective, the next stage is practical action. "فَرَاغَ عَلَيْهِمْ..."

3- Struggling against falsehood and reforming society requires power. "ضَرْبًا بِالْيَمِينِ"

4- Not every art is valuable. (In making the idols, sculptors had displayed artistic skill, but Prophet Abraham smashed them. Yes, the grandeur of falsehood must be broken, even if costs and arts were employed for that falsehood. The appearance of polytheism (shirk) and superstitions holds no sanctity.) "ضَرْبًا بِالْيَمِينِ"

5- Both logic and power are necessary. "ضَرْبًا بِالْيَمِينِ... أَتَعْبُدُونَ"

6- Everything must be sacrificed for Monotheism (Tawhīd) and monotheistic thought. (Imam Husayn عليه السلام was sacrificed so that the tyrant (Tāghūt) would not rule over the people; all the efforts and even the lives of the Prophets were sacrificed for Monotheism. Here too, Abraham undertook a dangerous action to abolish idol worship.) "فَرَاغَ عَلَيْهِمْ... قَالُوا ابْنُوا لَهُ بُنْيَانًا"

7- Do not expect defeated enemies to sit quietly. The idolaters rushed towards Abraham with haste. "فَأَقْبَلُوا إِلَيْهِ يَزِفُّونَ"

8- Imprisonment and arrest do not prevent the Prophets from propagating their message. (They arrested Abraham and asked: Why did you break the idols? He began propagating Monotheism:) "أَتَعْبُدُونَ مَا تَنْحِتُونَ"

9- That which is made by human hands is not worthy of being worshipped by humans. "أَتَعْبُدُونَ مَا تَنْحِتُونَ"

10- Even the things made by human hands are, in reality, created by Allah. "خَلَقَكُمْ وَمَا تَعْمَلُونَ"

11- Idol worship lacks sound logic and reasoning, which is why the polytheists resort to force. "أَلْقُوهُ فِي الْجَحِيمِ"

12- Fanaticism and rigidity reach a point where an infallible Prophet like Abraham, who possesses eloquence, miracles, and knowledge, is condemned to be burned merely for the sake of a handful of stones, wood, and inanimate objects. "أَلْقُوهُ فِي الْجَحِيمِ"

13- If we fulfill our duty, Allah Himself will respond to the enemies' plots. "فَأَرَادُوا بِهِ كَيْدًا فَجَعَلْنَاهُمُ الْأَسْفَلِينَ"

14- The Will of Allah governs natural factors. "فَجَعَلْنَاهُمُ الْأَسْفَلِينَ"

Ayahs

وَقَالَ إِنِّى ذَاهِبٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّى سَيَهْدِينِ (۹۹)
He said, ‘Indeed I am going toward my Lord, who will guide me.’ (99)
رَبِّ هَبْ لِى مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱٠٠)
‘My Lord! Give me [an heir], one of the righteous.’ (100)
فَبَشَّرْنَـٰهُ بِغُلَـٰمٍ حَلِيمٍ (۱٠۱)
So We gave him the good news of a forbearing son. (101)
فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ ٱلسَّعْىَ قَالَ يَـٰبُنَىَّ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰ فِى ٱلْمَنَامِ أَنِّىٓ أَذْبَحُكَ فَٱنظُرْ مَاذَا تَرَىٰ ۚ قَالَ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ ٱفْعَلْ مَا تُؤْمَرُ ۖ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۱٠۲)
When he was old enough to assist in his endeavour, he said, ‘My son! I see in dreams that I am sacrificing you. See what you think.’ He said, ‘Father! Do whatever you have been commanded. If Allah wishes, you will find me to be patient.’ (102)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The intention of Prophet Abraham (Ibrahim) in going towards God was attention to Allah through worship and effort. (21) Indeed, the value of every movement lies in the destination towards which it is performed. In Surah Ash-Shu'ara, Ayah 83, where it says «ذاهب الى ربّى», Prophet Abraham asks Allah that he himself be joined with the righteous, while in this Ayah (Surah As-Saffat), he asks for a righteous son. Among all the prophets, only Abraham and Ishmael (Isma'il) have been described with forbearance (patience/clemency). «انّ ابراهیم لاوّاه حلیم» (22), «بغلام حلیم»

Several dreams are narrated in the Quran whose fulfillment in wakefulness is a sign of the soul's independence and its connection with unseen matters, including: a) The dream of Prophet Joseph (Yusuf) in which eleven stars, along with the moon and the sun, prostrated to him. b) The dream of the ruler of Egypt in which seven lean cows ate seven fat cows. c) The dream of the prisoners in Egypt which Joseph interpreted for them. d) The dream of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله that Muslims would enter the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram). e) The dream of Prophet Abraham in which he sacrifices his son.

21) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 22) Tawbah, 114.

Messages

1- Divine men do not reach a dead end; through reliance and attention to Allah, they find the way. «ذاهب الى ربّى سیهدین»

2- The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah is the cause of His guidance and opening of the way. «ربّى سیهدین»

3- Let us step forward on the path of Allah and be certain of His favors. «سیهدین» (Movement is from you, blessing is from Allah.)

4- Righteous offspring are a Divine gift and one of the prayers of the prophets. «هب لى من الصالحین»

5- What must be central in a child is righteousness, not other matters. «هب لى من الصالحین»

6- A righteous son is the best helper for the family's movement towards Allah. «ذاهب الى ربّى... ربّ هب لى من الصالحین»

7- One of the signs of a good son is helping the father in work and effort. «معه السّعى»

8- The dreams of the prophets are separate from the dreams of others, especially when the vision is repeated. «اَرى فى المنام» (The vision of the prophets is a form of revelation. It is also stated in a narration: «رؤیا الانبیاء وحى» (23))

9- In the family, the conversation between the father and children should be affectionate and respectful. «یا بنّى، یا ابتِ»

10- On the path of Allah, one must abandon all attachments, even attachment to one's child. (A son whom he had awaited for nearly a century and obtained from Allah through prayer and supplication, now that he has grown up and become a support for his elderly father, he must sacrifice him with his own hands.) «انّى اذبحك...»

11- To foster the growth and personality of your children, consult with them and ask for their opinion. «فانظر ماذا ترى »

12- Seeking the opinion of people is beneficial, even in clear and certain matters, to gauge their level of readiness. (Abraham had no doubt about his mission, yet He still presented the issue to Ishmael.) «فانظر ماذا ترى »

13- The believer is submissive to Allah and does not make excuses regarding His commands. «یا ابت افعل ما تؤمر» While Ishmael could have told his father: a) This is a dream, not wakefulness. b) Killing a son is forbidden (Haram). c) The command does not imply immediacy; wait. d) This is an advisory command (Irshadi), not a mandatory one (Mawlawi); it is a recommendation, not an obligation.

14- The younger generation should encourage and reassure the elders in performing Divine tasks. «یا ابت افعل ما تؤمر»

15- We should raise the motivation for performing tasks. (Ishmael does not say: «اذبحنى، اقتلنى» (Slaughter me, kill me), which would be difficult for his father, but rather says: «اِفعل ما تؤمر» (Do what you are commanded), implying: it is a Divine command conveyed to you, so carry it out.)

16- Acting upon duty requires patience and steadfastness, and patience must be sought from Allah. «ستجدنى ان شاء اللّهُ من الصابرین»

17- Patience and forbearance are characteristics of the righteous. «الصالحین - من الصابرین»

18- Ishmael's patience is the fulfillment of Allah's promise regarding his forbearance. «بغلام حلیم... من الصابرین»

19- We should attribute our perfections to God, not to ourselves. «ان شاء اللّهُ من الصابرین»

20- We should say "Insha'Allah" (If God wills) in our affairs. «ستجدنى ان شاء اللّهُ من الصابرین»

23) Tafsir Burhan.

Ayahs

فَلَمَّآ أَسْلَمَا وَتَلَّهُۥ لِلْجَبِينِ (۱٠۳)
So when they had both surrendered [to Allah’s will], and he had laid him down on his forehead, (103)
وَنَـٰدَيْنَـٰهُ أَن يَـٰٓإِبْرَٰهِيمُ (۱٠٤)
We called out to him, ‘O Abraham! (104)
قَدْ صَدَّقْتَ ٱلرُّءْيَآ ۚ إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱٠٥)
You have indeed fulfilled your vision! Thus indeed do We reward the virtuous! (105)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْبَلَـٰٓؤُا۟ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱٠٦)
This was indeed a manifest test.’ (106)

Key Points

“Tallah” means he laid him down upon the earth. “Jabeen” means one side of the face and the forehead. After the father and son (Abraham and Ishmael) both declared their readiness to carry out the Divine command, and Abraham laid his son upon his face, a call came: We did not desire the slaughter of Ishmael, but We desired your detachment from your son in the way of Allah, which you demonstrated.

The Importance of Motivation and Intention

In the culture of the Quran and the school of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, the primary value is given to the motivation and intention behind the action. To the extent that the intention of the believer to perform a good deed is considered more valuable than his action. “The intention of the believer is better than his action.” (24)

Many deeds that were not performed have been praised due to positive motivations, and many deeds that were performed have been criticized due to negative motivations.

Now we mention a few examples of deeds that were not performed but were praised and rewarded:

  • The action of Prophet Abraham in attempting to sacrifice his son. «قد صدقت الرؤیا»
  • Uways al-Qarani went to Medina to meet the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and although he did not succeed in meeting him, his visit was accepted. In contrast, there were individuals beside the Prophet who harmed him.
  • A group of the Prophet’s companions did not have the means to go to the battlefield, so they were distressed and weeping. (25)
  • Individuals whose evil deeds Allah transforms into good deeds. «یبدّل اللّه سیّئاتهم حسنات» (26)
  • Those who die with the love of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام receive the reward of a martyr. «من مات على حبّ آل محمّد مات شهیدا» (27)
  • A group who are granted multiplied rewards. «اضعافا مضاعفة»

However, good deeds that have been criticized:

  • Deeds performed with corrupt intention, accompanied by showing off (Riya) and vanity. «الّذینهم یُراؤن... ویل للمصلّین» (28)
  • A good deed accompanied by a corrupt action, such as charity accompanied by reminding the recipient of the favor (Mann) or harm. «لا تبطلوا صدقاتكم بالمنّ والاذى» (29)
  • The deeds of those about whom the Quran says: They imagine that they have value and that their work is good. «یحسبون انهم یحسنون صنعا» (30)

24) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 84. 25) Tawbah, 92. 26) Furqan, 70. 27) Bihar, Vol. 23, p. 233. 28) Ma’un, 4. 29) Baqarah, 264. 30) Kahf, 104.

Messages

1- An action is praiseworthy when it is accompanied by submission and contentment. «اسلما و تلّه»

2- In attaining spiritual perfection, even a youth can reach the level of seasoned, elderly saints. «اسلما»

3- The sign of true submission is immediate action. «فلمّا اسلما و تلّه...»

4- Patience in obedience makes a person submissive to the command of Allah. «من الصابرین... اسلما و تلّه»

5- Divine commands are sometimes for testing. (We wanted you to detach yourself, not for Ishmael’s blood to be shed). «قد صدقت الرؤیا»

6- More important than the action itself is the decision to act and the state of submission. «قد صدقت الرؤیا»

7- Divine favors are governed by law, not arbitrary or without reckoning. «كذلك نجزى المحسنین» (Whoever submits to Allah like Abraham and Ishmael will receive Divine rewards and favors.)

8- Detaching oneself from one's child is among the most difficult Divine tests. (The degree of faith and sincerity of Abraham and Ishmael was made clear in this trial.) «البلاء المبین»

Ayahs

وَفَدَيْنَـٰهُ بِذِبْحٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱٠۷)
Then We ransomed him with a great sacrifice, (107)
وَتَرَكْنَا عَلَيْهِ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۱٠۸)
and left for him a good name in posterity: (108)
سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ (۱٠۹)
‘Peace be to Abraham!’ (109)
كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۱٠)
Thus do We reward the virtuous. (110)
إِنَّهُۥ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۱۱)
He is indeed one of Our faithful servants. (111)
وَبَشَّرْنَـٰهُ بِإِسْحَـٰقَ نَبِيًّا مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱۱۲)
And We gave him the good news of [the birth of] Isaac, a prophet, one of the righteous. (112)
وَبَـٰرَكْنَا عَلَيْهِ وَعَلَىٰٓ إِسْحَـٰقَ ۚ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا مُحْسِنٌ وَظَالِمٌ لِّنَفْسِهِۦ مُبِينٌ (۱۱۳)
And We blessed him and Isaac. Among their descendants [some] are virtuous, and [some] who manifestly wrong themselves. (113)

Key Points

The words fidā' (فداء) and fidyah (فدیه) mean to die in place of someone, to avert calamity, and to ransom. Allah promised that the good name of Abraham would remain in history: «و تركنا علیه فى الآخرین» (And We left for him [a good name] among later generations). All the rituals of Hajj, from the circumambulation (Tawāf) of the Ka'bah to the prayer performed behind the Station of Abraham (Maqām Ibrāhīm), and the emergence of Prophets from his lineage—all of these are examples of his good remembrance throughout history.

Messages

1- In religious culture, sacrificing an animal is effective in averting danger. «و فدیناه بذبح عظیم»

2- Sacrifice is among the traditions of Abraham. «بذبح عظیم»

3- A single sincere spark turns into a great historical movement. «بذبح عظیم» Hundreds of thousands of sheep, cows, and camels are slaughtered and sacrificed annually at the place of sacrifice (Qurbāngāh) of Ishmael in his remembrance.

4- The permanence of a good name is among the divine favors granted to the men of God. «و تركنا...»

5- Dealing with Allah yields eternal profit. «و تركنا علیه فى الاخرین»

6- Allah sends salutations (peace) upon the Prophets. «سلام على ابراهیم» (Peace be upon Abraham), «سلام على نوح فى العالمین» (Peace be upon Noah among the worlds) (31), «سلام على موسى و هارون» (Peace be upon Moses and Aaron) (32). Of course, regarding the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, both Allah and the angels send blessings (Salawāt), and He also commands the believers to send blessings and salutations: «انّ اللّه و ملائكته یصلّون على النبىّ...» (Indeed, Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet...) (33).

7- Rewarding the doers of good is the tradition and program of Allah. «كذلك نجزى المحسنین»

8- Abraham is the model and example for the doers of good. «نجزى المحسنین»

9- Abraham possesses a blessed progeny. In prayer, we recite: O Allah! Just as You blessed him, bless Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad as well. «باركنا علیه وعلى اسحاق» (And We blessed him and Isaac).

10- Faith (Īmān) and goodness (Ihsān) are inseparable from one another. «المحسنین... المؤمنین» (the doers of good... the believers).

11- The value of a child lies in his being righteous. «و بشّرناه باسحاق نبیّاً من الصالحین»

12- It is possible for a child to become unworthy, even if they are from the family of the Prophets. «و من ذرّیتهما... ظالم لنفسه...» (And among their descendants... is one who wrongs himself...). Yes, kinship does not guarantee the guidance or misguidance of children. (It is possible for a Prophet's father or his child to be deviated).
