Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ مَنَنَّا عَلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ (۱۱٤)
Certainly We favoured Moses and Aaron, (114)
وَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُمَا وَقَوْمَهُمَا مِنَ ٱلْكَرْبِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۱۱٥)
and delivered them and their people from their great distress, (115)
وَنَصَرْنَـٰهُمْ فَكَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبِينَ (۱۱٦)
and We helped them so that they became the victors. (116)
وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ٱلْمُسْتَبِينَ (۱۱۷)
We gave them the illuminating scripture (117)
وَهَدَيْنَـٰهُمَا ٱلصِّرَٰطَ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمَ (۱۱۸)
and guided them to the straight path, (118)
وَتَرَكْنَا عَلَيْهِمَا فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۱۱۹)
and left for them a good name in posterity. (119)

Messages

1- Remembering Allah's graces upon previous prophets is a source of tranquility, encouragement, and consolation for the Prophet of Islam and the Muslims during the difficult conditions in Mecca. «ولقد مننّا»

2- The removal of psychological pressures is among the greatest Divine blessings. «مننّا... نجّینا... من الكرب العظیم»

3- Men of God are relieved of worry only when their people are also relieved. «و نجیناهما و قومها من الكرب العظیم»

4- Whoever receives the grace and help of Allah will certainly be victorious. «نصرناهم فكانوا هم الغالبین»

5- First, salvation from the tyrant (Taghut), then the invitation to the Book and the path of God. «نجّینا... آتیناهما الكتاب المستبین و هدیناهما»

6- The messages and contents of the Torah and other heavenly books are very clear and understandable to everyone. «الكتاب المستبین»

7- Speeches and writings related to the propagation of religion must be clear and illuminating. «الكتاب المستبین»

8- Even the prophets are in need of guidance from Allah. «هدیناهما الصراط المستقیم»

9- A good name is one of the Divine rewards in this world. «تركنا علیهما فى الاخرین» In this Surah, Prophet Nuh in Ayah 78, and Ibrahim عليه السلام in Ayah 107, and Musa and Harun علیهما السلام in Ayah 119 have been honored through the preservation of their good names.

Ayahs

سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ (۱۲٠)
‘Peace be to Moses and Aaron!’ (120)
إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۲۱)
Thus indeed do We reward the virtuous. (121)
إِنَّهُمَا مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۲۲)
They are indeed among Our faithful servants. (122)

Key Points

Wherever in this Surah He said that He left a good name for the prophets, He pointed to three elements: 1. Good deeds (Ihsan) 2. Faith (Iman) 3. Worship ('Ibadah). «انّا كذلك نجزى المحسنین انّهما من عبادنا المؤمنین»

Messages

1- Men of God are alive and receive greetings (salutations). «سلام على...»

2- In showing respect to others, hierarchy should be maintained. (First the greeting upon Moses, then Aaron).

3- The greeting of the greater (superior) to the lesser (subordinate) is desirable. (Allah greets His creation).

4- Propagating the religion and explaining Divine knowledge is a type of good deed (Ihsan). «موسى و هارون... المحسنین»

5- Moses and Aaron are among the models and examples of those who do good (the righteous). «نجزى المحسنین»

6- Divine favors upon the doers of good are a tradition (Sunnah) and an ongoing process. «كذلك نجزى المحسنین»

7- Encouraging righteous individuals is a necessity of management and a Divine tradition. «كذلك نجزى المحسنین»

8- Faith (Iman) and good deeds (Ihsan) are inseparable from one another. «المحسنین - المؤمنین»

9- The path to receiving Divine favors is faith in Him and servitude to Him. «من عبادنا المؤمنین»

Ayahs

وَإِنَّ إِلْيَاسَ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۲۳)
Indeed Ilyas was one of the apostles. (123)
إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۱۲٤)
When he said to his people, ‘Will you not be Godwary? (124)
أَتَدْعُونَ بَعْلًا وَتَذَرُونَ أَحْسَنَ ٱلْخَـٰلِقِينَ (۱۲٥)
Do you invoke Baal and abandon the best of creators, (125)
ٱللَّهَ رَبَّكُمْ وَرَبَّ ءَابَآئِكُمُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۲٦)
Allah, your Lord and Lord of your forefathers?,’ (126)
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَمُحْضَرُونَ (۱۲۷)
they impugned him. So they will indeed be mustered [in hell] (127)
إِلَّا عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (۱۲۸)
—[all] except Allah’s exclusive servants. (128)
وَتَرَكْنَا عَلَيْهِ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۱۲۹)
We left for him a good name in posterity. (129)

Key Points

The word "Ba'l" (بَعل) is used for a husband. Idolaters referred to some of their idols, which they considered superior to themselves, as Ba'l.

Messages

1- Let us not forget the names, memories, and efforts of the prophets, and let us learn lessons from the history of their lives and their methods of dealing with their opponents and supporters. «ان الیاس لمن المرسلین»

2- The sending of prophets (Bi'that) is a divine tradition (Sunnah). «لمن المرسلین»

3- The forefront of the prophets' call and the foundation of all perfections is piety (Taqwa) and God-fearingness. «الا تتقون»

4- Whoever possesses piety abandons illogical beliefs. «الا تتقون اتدعون بعلاً»

5- Comparing the idol with God awakens every sleeping conscience. «أتدعون بعلاً و تذرون احسن الخالقین»

6- The most excellent system (Nizam Ahsan) governs existence. «احسن الخالقین»

7- Worship is worthy of the Best of Creators. «تذرون احسن الخالقین»

8- Sincere servitude ('Ubūdiyyah) is the cause of salvation from divine reckoning and punishment on the Day of Judgment. «فانهم لمحضرون الا عباد اللّه المخلصین»

Ayahs

سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَىٰٓ إِلْ يَاسِينَ (۱۳٠)
‘Peace be to Ilyas!’ (130)
إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۳۱)
Thus indeed do We reward the virtuous. (131)
إِنَّهُۥ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۳۲)
He is indeed one of Our faithful servants. (132)

Key Points

The intent behind «اِلیاسین» (Ilyasin) is the same as «الیاس» (Ilyas), about whom the discussion took place a few verses earlier. These two words are the name of one individual, just like «سینا» (Sina) and «سینین» (Sinin) are the names of one land. The best proof for this matter is, firstly, that the singular pronoun is used for him in the subsequent verses: «انّه من عبادنا» (Indeed, He is one of Our servants); and secondly, the similarity and repetition of these verses concerning the previous Prophets—namely Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Aaron—indicate that the intent behind «اِلیاسین» in «سلام على الیاسین» (Peace be upon Ilyasin) is the same as «الیاس» in «انّ الیاس لمن المرسلین» (Indeed, Ilyas was among the messengers). Narrations that read «اِلیاسین» as «آل یاسین» (Aal Yasin) and interpret it as referring to the Household of the Prophet of Islam علیهم السلام are mostly not from the Ahl al-Bayt and are not reliable. The repetition of the verse «انّا كذلك نجزى المحسنین» (Indeed, thus do We reward the doers of good) in this Surah is to encourage conformity with the Prophets, who are referred to as muhsin (doers of good).

Messages

1- Let us learn from Allah how to send greetings (salutations) upon the Prophets, the Awliya (saints), and the great figures. «سلام على اِل یاسین»

2- Kindness and favor toward the doers of good is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah and is perpetually ongoing. «كذلك نجزى المحسنین»

3- The criterion for receiving salutations from Allah is goodness (Ihsan) coupled with faith and servitude. «سلام على... المحسنین... عبادنا المؤمنین»

4- Whoever is a religious propagator, like Ilyas, is among the muhsinun (doers of good) and recipients of divine rewards. «الیاس لمن المرسلین... سلام على اِل یاسین... المحسنین»

Ayahs

وَإِنَّ لُوطًا لَّمِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۳۳)
Indeed Lot was one of the apostles. (133)
إِذْ نَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱۳٤)
When We delivered him and all his family, (134)
إِلَّا عَجُوزًا فِى ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (۱۳٥)
excepting an old woman among those who remained behind, (135)
ثُمَّ دَمَّرْنَا ٱلْـَٔاخَرِينَ (۱۳٦)
We destroyed the rest. (136)
وَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهِم مُّصْبِحِينَ (۱۳۷)
Indeed you pass by them at dawn (137)
وَبِٱلَّيْلِ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۱۳۸)
and at night. Do you not exercise your reason? (138)

Key Points

The word ghābir (غابر) means 'one who remains behind,' just as ghubār (غبار) is the residue of dust. ‘Ajūz (عجوز) refers to an old woman who is incapable of work and effort. The word dammarnā (دَمّرنا) is derived from tadmir (تدمیر), meaning 'to destroy' or 'to annihilate.' The ruined region of the people of Lot was, in the early days of Islam, on the route between Hijaz and Sham (Syria), which caravans and travelers passed by every morning and evening. Non-religious psychologists name factors that compulsorily shape human personality, stating, for example: "The social or economic system is the shaper of man." However, Islam considers the axis of development to be the human being's own will, not the external conditions governing him. For instance, the wife of Pharaoh sat at Pharaoh's table and lived in the palace, yet she was not affected by him in the slightest. Conversely, the wives of Lot and Noah lived in the homes of these two Prophets but followed a different path.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the painful and arduous history of the Prophets is a source of solace for the Prophet and steadfastness for the believers. «و ان لوطاً لمن المرسلین»

2- Allah gives everyone punishment and reward according to their deeds. «نجّیناه... الا عجوزاً»

3- Physical connection is not a cause for salvation; intellectual connection is necessary. «الا عجوزاً»

4- The account of the Prophets is separate from the account of their wives. «نجّیناه... الاّ عجوزاً»

5- The Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophets are the intellectual and practical followers of those great figures, not merely their family members. «نجّیناه و اهله اجمعین الاّ عجوزاً»

6- If there is no spiritual growth involved, the wife of a Prophet is no different from other individuals in the slightest. «فى الغابرین»

7- Salvation and destruction are in the hands of Allah. «نجّینا - دمّرنا»

8- We should not pass by ruins and ancient sites simply and carelessly. «أفلا تعقلون»

9- The story of the people of Lot is a source of lesson (or warning) for everyone. «أفلا تعقلون»

Ayahs

وَإِنَّ يُونُسَ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۳۹)
Indeed Jonah was one of the apostles. (139)
إِذْ أَبَقَ إِلَى ٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلْمَشْحُونِ (۱٤٠)
When he absconded toward the laden ship, (140)
فَسَاهَمَ فَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُدْحَضِينَ (۱٤۱)
he drew lots with them and was the one to be condemned [as one to be thrown overboard]. (141)
فَٱلْتَقَمَهُ ٱلْحُوتُ وَهُوَ مُلِيمٌ (۱٤۲)
Then the fish swallowed him while he was blameworthy. (142)
فَلَوْلَآ أَنَّهُۥ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسَبِّحِينَ (۱٤۳)
Had he not been one of those who celebrate Allah’s glory, (143)
لَلَبِثَ فِى بَطْنِهِۦٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ (۱٤٤)
he would have surely remained in its belly till the day they will be resurrected. (144)

Key Points

The term 'abd 'ābiq (عبدِ «آبق») refers to a runaway slave who flees without the permission of his master. Prophet Yunus (عليه السلام) left his people without Allah's permission and headed into the wilderness. Sāham (ساهم) is derived from sahm (سهم), meaning drawing lots or casting a ballot. It is said that a whale obstructed the ship's movement, and they realized that if one person was not sacrificed, the danger would threaten all the passengers. Therefore, through drawing lots, they cast Yunus into the mouth of the whale so that the danger might be averted. Mudḥaḍīn (مُدحضین) is derived from idḥāḍ (اِدحاض), meaning to cause to slip, and in this context, it is an allusion to defeat and being overcome in the matter of drawing lots. Ḥūt (حوت) is used for a fish, whether large or small, but it is mostly applied to a large fish. If Allah commands His Prophet to leave and distance himself from the people, distancing oneself is a virtue, just as He told Lot: «فَاَسر باهلك...» (34). But if such an action occurs without Allah's permission, it is considered an anti-value and a flight (abaqa). A story must be short, varied, and beneficial. In this Surah, only a few verses are dedicated to the story of each Prophet. Yunus fled from his people due to his lack of patience, and thus he was not set as a model for subsequent Prophets. Allah tells the Prophet of Islam: Do not be like Yunus: «لا تكن كصاحب الحوت» (35). His story is also narrated at the end of this Surah.

34) Hud, 81. 35) Al-Qalam, 48.

Messages

  1. Familiarity with the history of the Prophets is a lesson for us today. «و اِنّ یونس لمن المرسلین»
  2. Drawing lots among individuals is a legitimate and historically established practice. «فساهم»
  3. Animals are sometimes commissioned by Allah to perform a task. «فالتقمه الحوت»
  4. Fleeing from responsibility is not a solution. (Yunus left his people, but his situation worsened). «فالتقمه الحوت و هو ملیم»
  5. Some of our life problems stem from negligence in fulfilling responsibility. «ابق الى الفلك... فالتقمه الحوت»
  6. Sometimes an ultimatum is necessary. «لولا انه كان من المسبّحین...»
  7. Changing circumstances should not separate us from God (Allah). «من المسبّحین»
  8. Glorifying Allah (Tasbīḥ) is a factor in deliverance from distress. Prophet Yunus (عليه السلام) said inside the belly of the fish: «سبحانك انّى كنت من الظالمین» (36), «كان من المسبّحین»
  9. A brilliant track record is one of the factors of salvation. (Pharaoh and Yunus both confessed while in the water, but Pharaoh drowned while Yunus was freed, because the former had a bad record and the latter had a good record). «المرسلین - المسبّحین»
  10. By the Will of Allah, a human being can remain alive in the belly of a fish until the Day they are resurrected. «للبث فى بطنه الى یوم یبعثون» Therefore, a long lifespan, such as the age of Imam Mahdi (عليه السلام), is far from being improbable.

36) Al-Anbiya, 87.

Ayahs

۞ فَنَبَذْنَـٰهُ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ سَقِيمٌ (۱٤٥)
Then We cast him on a bare shore, and he was sick. (145)
وَأَنۢبَتْنَا عَلَيْهِ شَجَرَةً مِّن يَقْطِينٍ (۱٤٦)
So We made a gourd plant grow above him. (146)
وَأَرْسَلْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ مِا۟ئَةِ أَلْفٍ أَوْ يَزِيدُونَ (۱٤۷)
We sent him to a [community of] hundred thousand or more, (147)
فَـَٔامَنُوا۟ فَمَتَّعْنَـٰهُمْ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (۱٤۸)
and they believed [in him]. So We provided for them for a while. (148)

Key Points

Nabadh (نَبَذ) means to throw or cast away, and ‘Arā’ (عراء) means a barren land without vegetation. The word shajar (شجر) refers both to a tree and to a bush. Yaqtīn (یقطین) means gourd (or pumpkin), whose leaves are a source of remedy for bruises and insect repellent. Numbers in the Quran are sometimes of specific importance, such as in the Ayah, «ولیالٍ عشر» (37), which refers to ten nights, neither fewer nor more. Sometimes the number signifies multiplicity, such as in the Ayah: «لو یُعمر الف سنة» (38). Some Jews desire to live a thousand years, where the thousand signifies multiplicity. Sometimes the number in the Quran is used approximately, such as in the Ayah under discussion, which states that the people of Yunus were a hundred thousand or more. «مائة الفٍ او یزیدون»

In a Hadith from Imam Baqir عليه السلام, we read that Prophet Yunus was imprisoned in the belly of the fish for several days; his hair had fallen out and his skin had become thin. After exiting the belly of the fish, he sucked on the gourd plant and used its shade. (39)

Allah protected four Prophets: Musa (Moses) and Yusuf (Joseph) beside the water, Nuh (Noah) upon the water, and Yunus (Jonah) beneath the water. «فالتقمه الحوت فنبذناه»

37) Fajr, 2. 38) Baqarah, 96. 39) Tafasir Qummi and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Reproach should not prevent affection. Yunus was reproached and disciplined, but he was still granted Allah’s special favors. «و هو ملیم، انبتنا، ارسلنا...»

2- We should not neglect herbal medicines. «شجرة من یقطین»

3- Causing the gourd to grow in barren land (al-‘Arā’) is a sign of Divine power and assistance. «انبتنا... شجرة من یقطین»

4- In management, instead of dismissing personnel, we should rehabilitate them and employ them again. «و ارسلناه الى مائة الفٍ»

5- The scope of the Prophets' mission (Bi'that) is by Allah's choice and command. (Some are sent to all people, and some to a specific region.) «و ارسلناه الى مائة الفٍ»

6- The supplication and glorification (tasbīh) of Yunus عليه السلام became the cause for receiving Allah’s grace and assistance. «من المسبحین... انبتنا علیه...»

7- Fleeing does not necessitate not returning. Yunus had fled, but he returned to his own people and delivered his message. (Yes, sometimes a person gets angry, resigns, or divorces his wife, but as soon as he sees that returning is the expedient course, he must return.) «ابق الى الفلك... ارسلناه الى مائة الفٍ»

8- Repentance for the past is the foundation for success in the future. The people of Yunus repented, and Allah withdrew His wrath and made them successful. «فآمنوا فمتّعناهم»

Ayahs

فَٱسْتَفْتِهِمْ أَلِرَبِّكَ ٱلْبَنَاتُ وَلَهُمُ ٱلْبَنُونَ (۱٤۹)
Ask them, are daughters to be for your Lord while sons are to be for them? (149)
أَمْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ إِنَـٰثًا وَهُمْ شَـٰهِدُونَ (۱٥٠)
Did We create the angels females while they were present? (150)
أَلَآ إِنَّهُم مِّنْ إِفْكِهِمْ لَيَقُولُونَ (۱٥۱)
Be aware that it is out of their mendacity that they say, (151)
وَلَدَ ٱللَّهُ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكَـٰذِبُونَ (۱٥۲)
‘Allah has begotten [offsprings],’ and they indeed speak a falsehood. (152)
أَصْطَفَى ٱلْبَنَاتِ عَلَى ٱلْبَنِينَ (۱٥۳)
Has He preferred daughters to sons? (153)
مَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ (۱٥٤)
What is the matter with you? How do you judge? (154)
أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۱٥٥)
Will you not then take admonition? (155)
أَمْ لَكُمْ سُلْطَـٰنٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱٥٦)
Do you have a manifest authority? (156)
فَأْتُوا۟ بِكِتَـٰبِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱٥۷)
Then produce your scripture, should you be truthful. (157)

Messages

1- Questioning the conscience is the key to people's reflection and awareness. «فاستفتهم ألربّك البنات»

2- Some beliefs are so illogical and hollow that a moment of thought is enough to realize their invalidity. «ألربّك البنات و لهم البنون»

3- Considering angels to be female is subject to divine accountability. «ام خلقنا الملائكة اناثاً»

4- One who lacks knowledge has no right to express an opinion. «ام هم شاهدون»

5- The beliefs of the polytheists are fabricated and false. «من افكهم لیقولون»

6- We should not tell people about the superstitious beliefs of others so that they may better appreciate the value of divine guidance. «لیقولون ولد اللّه...»

7- Those who consider Christ to be the son of God are like the polytheists who considered the angels to be the children of God. «لیقولون ولد اللّه»

8- Female and male children are equal, and neither has superiority over the other. «اصطفى البنات على البنین»

9- Man reaches a point where, based on a false belief, he considers the daughter bad and the son good, and then assigns the daughters to God and keeps the sons for himself. «اصطفى البنات على البنین»

10- Beliefs must either be based on intellect, «كیف تحكمون» or based on innate nature (Fitrah). «أفلا تذكّرون» (Tadhakkur [remembrance] is the recalling of something that is in man's innate nature or based on documentation and transmission. «ام لكم سلطان مبین فأتوا بكتابكم»)

11- Heavenly Books are the proof and the basis for judgment concerning God, and none of these books mentions God having a child. «فأتوا بكتابكم»

Ayahs

وَجَعَلُوا۟ بَيْنَهُۥ وَبَيْنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ نَسَبًا ۚ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمَتِ ٱلْجِنَّةُ إِنَّهُمْ لَمُحْضَرُونَ (۱٥۸)
And they have set up a kinship between Him and the jinn, while the jinn certainly know that they will be presented [before Him]. (158)
سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (۱٥۹)
Clear is Allah of whatever they allege [about Him] (159)
إِلَّا عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (۱٦٠)
—[all] except Allah’s exclusive servants. (160)

Key Points

Regarding what is meant by the relationship between Allah and the Jinn, there are two theories: 1. That the Jinn are partners of Allah, just as we read in Ayah 100 of Surah Al-An'am: «و جعلوا للّه شركاء الجّن» (And they made the Jinn partners with Allah). 2. That Allah has taken wives from the Jinn, from whom children like the angels have come into existence.

Messages

  1. A person who separates himself from logic and proof attributes any superstition and slander to whomever he wishes, even going so far as to imagine a wife for Allah from the species of Jinn. «جعلوا بینه و بین الجنة...»

  2. The Jinn are intelligent and responsible beings (mukallaf), and they believe in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and in being present before the Divine Justice for accountability. «علمت الجنّ انهم لمحضرون»

  3. Everyone will be interrogated before Allah, and Allah has no kinship with anyone. If the Jinn had a relationship with Allah, they would not be summoned before Him for punishment and reckoning. «انّهم لمحضرون»

  4. Wherever superstitious talk about Allah arises, declare the Divine Essence pure (tanzeeh) with the phrase «سبحان اللّه». «و جعلوا بینه... سبحان اللّه عما یصفون»

  5. Wherever the speech of the misguided and deviated is mentioned, separate the account of the pure servants. (The phrase «الاّ عباداللّه المخلصین» has been repeated many times in this Surah).

  6. All human beings, except for the chosen servants of Allah, are incapable of describing Him appropriately. «سبحان اللّه عما یصفون الاّ عباد اللّه المخلصین»

Ayahs

فَإِنَّكُمْ وَمَا تَعْبُدُونَ (۱٦۱)
Indeed you and what you worship (161)
مَآ أَنتُمْ عَلَيْهِ بِفَـٰتِنِينَ (۱٦۲)
cannot mislead [anyone] about Him, (162)
إِلَّا مَنْ هُوَ صَالِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱٦۳)
except someone who is bound for hell. (163)

Key Points

The word "fātin" (فاتن) is derived from "fitnah" (فتنه), meaning tempter or deceiver. The religion of polytheism (shirk) and idol worship is powerless against Monotheism (Tawhid), and even if it has followers today, it will soon reach a dead end in the near future. The word "ṣāl" (صال) is derived from "ṣālī" (صالى), referring to someone who, by choice and selection, adheres closely to something.

Messages

1- Allah created man free, and it is he who chooses his path; idolaters and their idols have no power of compulsion. «ما انتم علیه بفاتنین»

2- Only individuals of evil nature and those who flee from the truth are influenced by the polytheists and the idols. (Polytheists and idols cannot deceive people or rise up against Monotheism, except for those who voluntarily choose the deviant path of polytheism.) «الاّ من هو صال الجحیم»

Ayahs

وَمَا مِنَّآ إِلَّا لَهُۥ مَقَامٌ مَّعْلُومٌ (۱٦٤)
‘There is none among us but has a known place. (164)
وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلصَّآفُّونَ (۱٦٥)
It is we who are the ranged ones. (165)
وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلْمُسَبِّحُونَ (۱٦٦)
It is we who celebrate Allah’s glory.’ (166)

Key Points

It is possible that the intent of the phrase «مقام معلوم» (known station) here refers to the station of the sincere ones, which was mentioned in the preceding verses: «الاّ عباد اللّه المخلصین» (40). This means that for every sincere and chosen servant of Allah, there is a specific station and rank. Similarly, some narrations have identified the infallible Imams and the divine successors (Awsiya) as the manifestation (misdaq) of this verse (41).

(40) Saffat, 160. (41) Tafasir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Rahnamā.

Messages

1- In the world of angels, there is a specific station, rank, and responsibility for each one, and a hierarchy exists. «مقام معلوم» (known station)

2- Angels are not the children of God, but rather divine agents who stand in disciplined ranks to execute the command of Allah. «الصّافون» (those who stand in ranks)

Ayahs

وَإِن كَانُوا۟ لَيَقُولُونَ (۱٦۷)
They indeed, used to say, (167)
لَوْ أَنَّ عِندَنَا ذِكْرًا مِّنَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱٦۸)
‘Had we possessed a Reminder from our predecessors, (168)
لَكُنَّا عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (۱٦۹)
we would have surely been Allah’s exclusive servants.’ (169)
فَكَفَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۷٠)
But they disbelieved it [when it came to them]. Soon they will know! (170)

Key Points

It is narrated from Imam Baqir عليه السلام that the polytheists of Mecca and the disbelievers of Quraysh used to curse the Jews and Christians for how they denied their own prophets, saying: "By God, if one of the previous heavenly Books were with us, we would certainly believe in it." But as soon as Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله was commissioned (as a prophet), they disbelieved in Him. (42) Perhaps the meaning of the Ayah is that the polytheists constantly said that if they had news of the previous monotheists being in happiness and bliss, they too would become people of Tawhid (monotheism). Thus, they evaded (the truth) and became disbelievers.

42) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Claims are numerous, and claimants are abundant. The believer is distinguished from the disbeliever at the time of action. «لو انّ عندنا... لكنّا عباد اللّه... فكفروا به»

2- In the face of logic and evidence, one should not use past history as an excuse. «لو ان عندنا ذكرا من الاولین...»

3- We must consider the outcome of our deeds, as heedlessness regarding the future leads to disbelief and denial. «فكفروا به فسوف یعلمون»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ سَبَقَتْ كَلِمَتُنَا لِعِبَادِنَا ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۷۱)
Certainly Our decree has gone beforehand in favour of Our servants, the apostles, (171)
إِنَّهُمْ لَهُمُ ٱلْمَنصُورُونَ (۱۷۲)
that they will indeed receive [Allah’s] help, (172)
وَإِنَّ جُندَنَا لَهُمُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبُونَ (۱۷۳)
and indeed Our hosts will be the victors. (173)

Key Points

In this Surah, the stories of Prophets such as Noah, Abraham, Moses, Aaron, Elijah, Lot, and Jonah were mentioned. This Ayah serves as a resolution and the conclusion of the work of those great figures, providing comfort to the Prophet and encouragement to the Muslims. Question: Although Allah has promised victory and assistance to the men of God, why do we sometimes witness their defeat? Answer: Firstly, those divine promises have been fulfilled, and today the number of followers of heavenly religions is far greater than the followers of human schools of thought. The opponents of the Prophets have perished, and there is no trace or sign of them. But the names of the Prophets are alive, and the places attributed to them are respected by the people. Their popularity and sanctity among the people cannot be compared with any of the scientists, artists, writers, poets, or politicians throughout history. Secondly; their defeats have always resulted from the shortcomings and weakness of their followers in carrying out their responsibilities, not from the lack of divine assistance and aid. Just as the defeat of the Muslims in the Battle of Uhud resulted from their weakness, disagreement, and disobedience. Thirdly; let us not forget the scientific, logical, and intellectual victory. Fourthly; let us not forget the destruction and divine wrath concerning the opponents. Fifthly; the age of the world is not over, and with the coming and appearance of Hazrat Mahdi, all the goals of truth will be realized.

Messages

1- The assistance granted to the Prophets is a divine tradition. «سبقت كلمتنا»

2- The future of humanity lies with the victory of the Prophets and the defeat of their enemies. «لقد سبقت كلمتنا»

3- Servitude and worship of Allah have been the condition for the saints (Awliya) to attain the rank of prophethood (Risalah). «لعبادنا المرسلین»

4- The victory of the Prophets and their school of thought over other schools of thought is certain. «انهم لهم المنصورون» (The particle inna, the particle lam, and the nominal sentence are signs of certainty.)

5- Servitude to Allah is the basis for receiving divine assistance. «لعبادنا... المنصورون»

6- The Prophets and their followers are the army of Allah, and the army of Allah is certainly victorious in all arenas. «انّ جندنا لهم الغالبون»

Ayahs

فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ (۱۷٤)
So leave them alone for a while, (174)
وَأَبْصِرْهُمْ فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ (۱۷٥)
and watch them; soon they will see [the truth of the matter]! (175)
أَفَبِعَذَابِنَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (۱۷٦)
Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? (176)
فَإِذَا نَزَلَ بِسَاحَتِهِمْ فَسَآءَ صَبَاحُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (۱۷۷)
But when it descends in their courtyard it will be a dismal dawn for those who had been warned. (177)
وَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ (۱۷۸)
So leave them alone for a while, (178)
وَأَبْصِرْ فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ (۱۷۹)
and watch; soon they will see! (179)
سُبْحَـٰنَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ ٱلْعِزَّةِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ (۱۸٠)
Clear is your Lord, the Lord of Might, of whatever they allege [concerning Him]. (180)
وَسَلَـٰمٌ عَلَى ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۸۱)
Peace be to the apostles! (181)
وَٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۸۲)
All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds. (182)

Messages

1- The educator and leader must sometimes turn away from stubborn and misguided people as a form of threat and protest. «فتولّ عنهم»

2- He whose victory is guaranteed by the promises of Allah must act from a position of strength. «فتولّ عنهم»

3- Turning away (aversion) and anger must be logical, divine, and temporary (not vengeful, ego-driven, or permanent). «حتّى حین»

4- Repetition is necessary in the face of the disbelievers' disbelief regarding divine threats. The command «تولّ عنهم... وابصر» has been repeated twice.

5- First, warning, reminder, and completion of the proof (Hujjah); then, wrath and punishment. «فاذا نزل بساحتهم» occurs while they have already been warned. «المنذرین»

6- Allah will make the obstinate taste the bitter flavor of defeat in this very world, and you believers will witness their ultimate fate. «و ابصرهم فسوف یبصرون»

7- All honor (might/glory) belongs to Allah. «ربّ العزّة»

8- The attributions made by the polytheists to Allah and their descriptions of Him are incompatible with Allah's status of Might (Izzah) and Lordship (Rububiyyah). «سبحان ربّك ربّ العزّة»

9- Allah is the source of all perfections and beauties. «الحمد للّه»

10- Only Allah is worthy of praise. «الحمد للّه»

11- Allah is the Owner and Administrator of the entire existence. «ربّ العالمین»

12- The entire existence is moving towards perfection and growth. «ربّ العالمین»

13- The universe of creation is a manifestation and display of Allah's Lordship, and Allah's Lordship necessitates praise and glorification.
